observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct the Local IATF to continuously enforce the existing health protocols implemented in the community and ensure that adequate health consciousness is instilled among its people.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) request the Sangguniang Bayan for the passing of ordinances legislating the operation of its LEEs; and (b) instruct the (i) Municipal Accountant to maintain special accounts for and render reports on the LEEs’ financial operations; (ii) Municipal Treasurer to cause the strict enforcement of the 2019 RCC; and (iii) Municipal Finance Committee to evaluate the LEEs’ performance to determine their financial viability and take actions in accordance with regulations.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) officials concerned to (i) secure the written acquiescence of the co-owner of TCT No. 051-2017001428 on the sale made; (ii) facilitate the titling of the lots in the name of the Municipality of Baler; and (iii) explain the inconsistencies noted in the documents supporting the sale; and (b) Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents as required under COA Circular No. 2012-01.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Human Resource Management Officer to initiate the filling up of the vacant positions at the LDRRMO; and (b) while hiring of said personnel is still in process, designate a planning officer therefor.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) Municipal Planning and Development Officer to monitor the efficient implementation of the projects under the 20% Development Fund so that the necessary interventions by the implementing offices may be made for their timely completion; and (b) ensure that development projects have all been subjected to thorough study and coordinated with other Government Agencies for their timely implementation.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct GAD Focal Person to (a) perform the GMEF to assess the gender-responsiveness of the agency policies and identified major programs and projects; (b) nominate the GFPS members for seminars/trainings on gender analysis to familiarize themselves with the gender mainstreaming; and (c) in the absence clear indicator when the Covid pandemic will be eradicated, properly craft GAD activities that can be implemented even with the existing health protocols.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continuously withhold taxes from employees’ compensations and payments to suppliers and remit the same to BIR in accordance with existing revenue regulations.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to advise the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continuously comply with the existing rules and regulation on the withholding and remittance of GSIS contributions and loan amortization due from employees including the mandatory employer’s share, and in performing the required reportorial functions.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continuously comply with the existing rules and regulation on the withholding and remittance of HDMF contribution from employees including the mandatory employer’s share, and in performing the required reportorial functions.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continuously comply with the existing rules and regulation on the withholding and remittance of Philhealth contributions due from employees including mandatory employer’s share and in performing the required reportorial functions.
We recommended and the LCE agreed to cause the settlement of the audit suspensions and disallowances in accordance with Section 9.4 and 10.4 of the RRSA.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the designated General Service Officer to undertake the (a) annual registration with the LTO of all its motor vehicles, simultaneous with placing the same under GSIS insurance coverage; (b) refrain from authorizing the use of said vehicles unless these are duly registered and properly insured; and (c) provide proof of ownership on the seven vehicles as identified in audit.
observations.reason: Seven motor vehicles remained unregistered and still on the process of securing registration.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) BAC to strictly comply with the provisions of GPPB Resolution 03-2020 and the related subsequent issuances on COVID expenditures; and (b) ensure that correct taxes are withheld.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require all officials involved in the planning for and implementation development projects to ensure that (a) the limited funds appropriated for current year PPAs are optimally utilized; and (b) future realignments of funds are duly sanctioned by the Sangguniang Bayan.
observations.reason: Project implementation was only 75.50% and waiting for the completion of DPWH project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct (a) the MDRRMO Head, Municipal Planning and Development Council and Municipal Engineer to evaluate the responsiveness of DRRM activities in preventing and mitigating disasters, and act accordingly on any deficiency noted; and (b) Municipal Accountant to revert to the unappropriated surplus of the GF the unutilized Special Trust Fund from CY 2015.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to (a) strictly collect the applicable stall fees and surcharges, if any, from the legitimate stall holders or lessees; (b) maintain an updated  individual index card of every stall/space holders; (c) institute measure/control to fully collect the market entrance for market products as required by the Code; (d) strictly impose the regulation on the licensing and timely calibration of instrument of weight and measures; (e) renew the contract of every stall/space occupants in frequency prescribed by the Code; and (f) regularly conduct ocular inspection of the market to determine compliance with the Code and impose applicable penalty/charges on violators.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) instruct the Local Development Council and the offices concerned to (i) determine and accordingly address the causes of the non/delayed implementation of the projects; and (ii) as warranted suggest for the reversion of the unused project fund to the General Fund; and (b) ensure that government funds are timely and efficiently utilized to promote a facilitated delivery of public service.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) invest on its personnel in the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Office by providing them at least the necessary seminar/training as required in the manual; (b) prioritize the procurement of disaster related equipment; (c) allocate monetary resources for the upgrading and maintenance of the existing evacuation centers; and (d) in the construction of evacuation center as embodied in its 2019 AIP, make sure that it is compliant with Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) Memorandum Circular No. 122 dated August 8, 2018.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to regularly prepare cash forecast/analysis; (b) Bids and Award Committee and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to align the execution of projects, program and activities with the result of cash forecast/analysis; and (c) ensure the maximum utilization of government funds with the end view of enhancing the delivery of public service.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) facilitate the establishment of a waste disposal facility; (b) instruct the MENRO to (i) implement measures to reduce solid waste by diverting at least 25% of all solid waste from waste disposal facilities and to increase the same every three years; (ii) develop a marketing strategy for recycled and composted materials; and (iii) maximize the utilization of available Solid Waste Equipment.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to (a) strictly collect the applicable stall fees and surcharges, if any, from the legitimate stall holders or lessees; (b) maintain an updated  individual index card of every stall/space holders; (c) institute measure/control to fully collect the market entrance for market products as required by the Code; (d) strictly impose the regulation on the licensing and timely calibration of instrument of weight and measures; (e) renew the contract of every stall/space occupants in frequency prescribed by the Code; and (f) regularly conduct ocular inspection of the market to determine compliance with the Code and impose applicable penalty/charges on violators.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor together with the Local Finance Committee to properly, monitor review and examine the implementation of the PPAs funded under the 20% Development Fund (DF) and make necessary adjustment/reprograming of PPA that cannot be implemented during the budget year.
observations.reason: DF totaling P7,031,851.90 remains not utilized/implemented at year end

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal mayor instruct the LDRRMO to (a) observe the provision of paragraph 6.5 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular on the use of prior years’ unexpended LDRRMF; and (b) regularly review and evaluate Annual LDRRM Plan and Budget, other dedicated DRRM resources, and other regular funding source/s and budgetary support of the LDRRMO.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor (a) fast track the establishment of the long
overdue sanitary landfill (b) continuously expand the coverage of waste collection area
observations.reason:
Sanitary landfill not yet established and
the same coverage of waste collections.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management concurred that the Local Chief Executive and the Local School Board (a) prepare a comprehensive annual plan and budget for SEF with adequate consideration of the actual, realistic and more pressing needs of the public school sector; and (b) monitor the implementation of PPAs vis-à-vis the milestones and timelines set to ensure optimum use of the fund.
observations.reason: Various critical and compelling needs of the students were still not properly address

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the (a) respective heads of the implementing departments to regularly review the MOA for the implementation of various projects funded under fund transfers; and (b) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to remit the collections due to different National Government Agencies and refund any unused fund to the source agencies after completion of the projects.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor together with the Local Finance Committee to properly, monitor review and examine the implementation of the PPAs funded under the 20% Development Fund (DF) and make necessary adjustment/reprograming of PPA that cannot be implemented during the budget year to optimally utilized the fund.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal mayor instruct the LDRRMO to (a) observe the provision of paragraph 6.5 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular on the use of prior years’ unexpended LDRRMF; and (b) regularly review and evaluate Annual LDRRM Plan and Budget, other dedicated DRRM resources, and other regular funding source/s and budgetary support of the LDRRMO.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor (a) fast track the establishment of the long overdue sanitary landfill; and (b) continuously expand the coverage of waste collections area.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor  instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to collect from the suppliers the liquidated damages in accordance with regulations or with the provision of the contract, as warranted; and (b) Bid and Award Committee (BAC) to make proper study on the appropriate delivery period for each procurement activities to avoid unnecessary extension period.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continuously withhold taxes from employee’s compensation and payments to suppliers and remit the same to BIR in accordance with existing revenue regulations.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continuously comply with and adhere to  the existing rules and regulation on withholding GSIS contributions and loan amortization due from employees, remitting the same including mandatory employer’s share and performing the required reportorial functions.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continuously comply with and adhere to  the existing rules and regulation on withholding HDMF contribution from employees, remitting the same including mandatory employer’s share and performing the required reportorial functions.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continuously comply with and adhere to the existing rules and regulation on withholding Philhealth contributions due from employees, remitting the same including mandatory employer’s share and performing the required reportorial functions.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the settlement of the audit suspensions and disallowances in accordance with Section 9.4 and 10.4 of the RRSA

We recommended and management concurred that the Municipal Mayor together with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Budget Officer to: (a) ensure that future annual investment plans conform with the requirements of applicable rules and regulations to preclude disallowances during budget reviews; and
(b) factor in the observed weather conditions in the area in the setting of timelines  for project implementation.
observations.reason: Two PPA remains not implemented at year end

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor together with the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources officer (a) expand/extend the coverage of waste collection and provide adequate and appropriate garbage bins; (b) encourage partnership with ambulant junk buyer and junk shop operator in the collection and segregation of residual wastes; (c) exhort the cooperation and participation of the general public in environmental protection; and
(d) make the solid waste management fund exclusive  for use in SWM Projects, Programs and Activities.
observations.reason: Not all barangay are covered with daily waste collection.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management concurred that the Municipal Mayor together with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Budget Officer to: (a) ensure that future annual investment plans conform with the requirements of applicable rules and regulations to preclude disallowances during budget reviews; and (b) factor in the observed weather conditions in the area in the setting of timelines  for project implementation.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor together with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and GAD Focal Person: (a) ensure compliance with at least five (5) percent appropriation on GAD PPA’s; (b) identify and undertake PPAs that are responsive to gender issues; and (c) take adequate measures to improve the gender equality in public employment.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor (a) instruct the supplier to make immediate replacement of the five items found not conforming to the specifications set forth in the contract, without prejudice to the imposition of LD as warranted; (b) require the Acting Property Officer to (i) conduct proper monitoring/inspection of the items delivered specially during the warranty period; (ii) determine the cause of the noted defects on five installed units and act accordingly; and (ii) henceforth, reject deliveries that do not comply with the specification parameters of the PO/contract; (b) caution the Municipal Engineer to (i) refrain from certifying conformance to the parameters of the contract and recommending payments therefor unless that fact is ascertained to avoid liability on ensuing audit disallowances, and (ii) before project implementation, consider factors that may present incompatibility with the actual condition in the field to preclude unnecessary changes in project implementation and the resulting additional costs.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor together with the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources officer (a) expand/extend the coverage of waste collection and provide adequate and appropriate garbage bins; (b) encourage partnership with ambulant junk buyer and junk shop operator in the collection and segregation of residual wastes; (c) exhort the cooperation and participation of the general public in environmental protection; and (d) make the solid waste management fund exclusive  for use in SWM Projects, Programs and Activities.

We recommended and management concurred that the Local Chief Executive and the Local School Board (a) prepare a comprehensive annual plan and budget for SEF with adequate consideration of the actual, realistic and more pressing needs of the public school sector; and (b) monitor the implementation of PPAs vis-à-vis the milestones and timelines set to ensure optimum use of the fund.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continuously withhold taxes from employee’s compensation and payments to suppliers and remit the same to BIR in accordance with existing revenue regulations.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continuously comply with and adhere to  the existing rules and regulation on withholding GSIS contributions and loan amortization due from employees, remitting the same including mandatory employer’s share and performing the required reportorial functions.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continuously comply with and adhere to  the existing rules and regulation on withholding HDMF contribution from employees, remitting the same including mandatory employer’s share and performing the required reportorial functions.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continuously comply with and adhere to the existing rules and regulation on withholding Philhealth contributions due from employees, remitting the same including mandatory employer’s share and performing the required reportorial functions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) ensure the full or optimum utilization of the 20% Development Fund during the budget year; and (b) require the concerned officers to be prudent in overseeing the timely implementation of PPAs under their responsibility.
observations.managementAction: The utilization rate of 20% development fund ground to 15.35% from CY 2016 utilization rate of 43.65%
observations.reason: 47.61% of the proposed PPA was disallowed in the review of the Sangguniang Panlalawigan of which 37.34% and 62.66% of the disallowed PPA were only reconsidered and realigned on June 6, and July 4, 2017 respectively. The other PPA were started late in the year.

observations.recommendation: 2.25	We recommend that the local chief executive instruct the GFPS to properly plan and regularly monitor the implementation of GAD projects and activities and prioritize those activities that can be undertaken for the budget year that would address the identified GAD issues.
observations.managementAction: The utilization rate of GAD increases from 26.31% to 82.59%
observations.reason: Some activities were not implemented due to insufficient fund allocation, thus deferred, while other has over allocation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

(a)	coordinate efforts and implement the five basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment expressly stated under R.A. No. 9003 and local regulations Municipal Ordinance No.01 series of 2008; and

(b)	involve closely the Barangay officials in the strict implementation of SWM programs and projects and intensify the information campaign on the “no segregation, no collection policy and the “no-littering policy” among the residents and tourists.
observations.managementAction: 31 individual who violates environmental ordinance were given citation ticket, inspection of institution and business establishment were conducted by MENRO to strengthen the segregation of waste and established building for temporary residual containment area.
observations.reason: The establishment of sanitary landfill was still not implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Members of the Municipal Local School Board:

(a)	prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization and stop the practice of accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute; and

(b)	maximize the utilization of SEF to attain its objectives, monitor the implementation of the Project Program Activities (PPA’s), so that necessary interventions may be introduced at the earliest time.
observations.managementAction: Implemented close monitoring of SEF to ensure proper charging of PPA
observations.reason: Various critical and compelling needs of students were still not properly address such as repair of toilet, lavatory, repair of building, provision of electric fan and replacement of busted bulb.

observations.recommendation: 1.10	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) ensure the full or optimum utilization of the 20% Development Fund during the budget year; and (b) require the concerned officers to be prudent in overseeing the timely implementation of PPAs under their responsibility.

2.25	We recommend that the local chief executive instruct the GFPS to properly plan and regularly monitor the implementation of GAD projects and activities and prioritize those activities that can be undertaken for the budget year that would address the identified GAD issues.

4.10	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
(a)	instruct Bids and Award Committee (BAC) to observe strictly the rules and regulations on procurement and ensure that the winning bidders/suppliers meet the legal, technical and financial requirements of the procurements under competitive bidding before the recommendation to award the contract is transmitted to HOPE; and
(b)	initiate the conduct of investigation on the procurement of defective/dysfunctional second-hand service vehicles and determine the liability, if any, of the responsible officer/s.

5.8	We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BUB Focal Person of the respective implementing offices to strictly monitor the implementation of projects and take note of any gaps/issues for future references or subsequent administrative action.

6.15	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
(a)	coordinate efforts and implement the five basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment expressly stated under R.A. No. 9003 and local regulations Municipal Ordinance No.01 series of 2008; and
(b)	involve closely the Barangay officials in the strict implementation of SWM programs and projects and intensify the information campaign on the “no segregation, no collection policy and the “no-littering policy” among the residents and tourists.

7.9	We recommended that Members of the Municipal Local School Board:
(a)	prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization and stop the practice of accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute; and
(b)	maximize the utilization of SEF to attain its objectives, monitor the implementation of the Project Program Activities (PPA’s), so that necessary interventions may be introduced at the earliest time.

9.5	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continuously comply with and adhere to  the existing rules and regulation on withholding GSIS due from employees, remitting the same including mandatory employer’s share and performing the required reportorial functions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) instruct the Municipal Planning Development Officer (MPDO) to abide strictly with the guidelines on the planning and implementation of the projects/ activities funded under the 20% Development Fund in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 11, 2011 for the benefit of its constituents; and (b) require the Municipal Budget Officer and other concerned municipal officials to explain/ justify the use of P168,450.00 despite exclusion thereof from its annual budget.
observations.managementAction: The utilization rate of 20% development fund as of December 31, 2016 was 43.65% from 14.81% as of December 31, 2015 or an increase of 28.84%
observations.reason: The utilization of local counterpart fund to LPRAP depends on the pre-requisite and release of funds from National Government. Out of the 13 project approved for funding only 4 projects received funds. The lack of technical capacities, limited personnel and the time devoted of most department heads in the procurement activities attributed to the limited utilization of 20% Development Fund.

observations.managementAction: As of December 31, 2016, the Municipality of Baler remains without a sanitary landfill.
observations.reason: The problem with the right of way going to the proposed sanitary landfill hindered the establishment of landfill

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 

instruct the members of the Municipal Local School Board to prepare and cause the approval of the annual School Board Budget for SEF by prioritizing programs, projects and activities as stipulated in Section 1, Republic Act No. 5447, Sections 100 and 272 of R.A. No. 7160, Deped-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01, s. 1998 dated April 14, 1998, and No. 01-A, March 14, 2000; 

Stop the practice of utilizing the fund for payment of MOOE of the DepEd Office and other expenses not related to or included in the list of prioritized expenses chargeable against SEF; and 
 
instruct the Property Custodian to coordinate with the DepEd Property Office and HRMO to strengthen the controls on property accountability and to monitor the clearances of DepEd personnel who are retiring, resigning, reassignment or terminating their employment.
observations.managementAction: A non-priority expenditure totaling ₱418,206.50 were still charged to SEF as of December 31, 2016.
observations.reason: The approval of the Local School Board of non-priority expenditure and non-observance of the needs of public schools resulted to incurrence of non-priority expenses

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the acting Property Officer to coordinate with the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office for the submission of necessary documents relative to the receipt and distribution of the relief goods such as RIS, Invoice Receipt and Relief Distribution Sheets to facilitate the recording, accounting, preparation and submission of the required reports and the Municipal Accountant to comply with Section VII on the proper accounting and/or disclosure to the financial statements on cash and in-kind donations for DRRM  pursuant to pertinent provisions of  COA Circular No. 2014-002.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO continuously distributed relief goods without proper coordination with the property officer
observations.reason: Non-coordination with the designated Property officer and the Accounting Department on the receipt and disposition of relief goods.
